channel.title: Sachin Rai — Field Notes
channel.description: Field notes from where AI infrastructure meets enterprise selling.

item.title: Thirteen Agents in One Saturday.
item.description: How I built a 13-agent pipeline in a single Saturday. What worked, what broke, and what I'd do differently with AI-native architecture.

item.title: Most Second Brains Just Rotate.
item.description: Why most personal knowledge systems feel busy but don't compound — and the architectural change that makes them work.

item.title: Vault: 7.5 → 9.5
item.description: Five fixes that took my Obsidian vault from functional to genuinely useful. The changes that compound.

item.title: I Renamed Seven Agents.
item.description: Naming your AI agents matters more than you think. How function-first names changed how I think about my entire system.

item.title: Turning an Obsidian Vault into an LLM-Queryable Knowledge Base
item.description: Instead of retrieving from raw documents at query time, build and maintain a persistent wiki. How I built mine and what it cost.

item.title: Win Rate Lies.
item.description: Win rate is the most misleading metric in enterprise sales. What to measure instead.

item.title: The AI sales stack I'd build today.
item.description: The specific tools, order of operations, and integration points I'd use to build an AI-native sales stack in 2026.

item.title: 7 AI trends I'm tracking in 2026.
item.description: The seven AI trends that matter for enterprise and what each one means for how we sell and build.
